1	# Plan — create the "grand plan" markdown for automated czstack ↔ HCR coregistration
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	The project goal is to automate the coregistration that is currently manual (BigWarp). Prior sessions have built solid foundations (benchmark loader, pia surface estimation, anisotropy/expansion analysis, depth-from-surface coordinate) but **no automated registration candidate has been implemented yet**. The user wants a **persistent markdown** that enumerates candidate approaches, ordered by simplicity × likely success rate, so future sessions pick them up one by one and update results in-place.
6	
7	This plan file is a meta-plan. The deliverable is a new doc in the repo — ordering, candidate sketches, reuse pointers, validation rules — that becomes the single source of truth for what to attempt next.
8	
9	## What the grand plan builds on (verified during this session)
10	
11	**Production-ready utilities (under `dev_code/` and `manual workflow/`):**
12	- `benchmark_data_loader.load_subject()` — unified loader; handles 3 GFP+ sources (spot-count CSV, raw spot aggregation, intensity CSV).
13	- `benchmark_analysis.estimate_pia_surface_quantile_ceiling()` — canonical HCR pia surface (N22 default, validated on all 6 subjects, `above_frac ≤ 0.08 %`, `onset_depth 2.5–7.5 µm`).
14	- `benchmark_analysis.estimate_pia_surface_image_ceiling()` — CZ image-based surface (roughness ≤ 12 µm, tilt ≤ 3°).
15	- `benchmark_analysis.depth_from_surface()`, `depth_profile()` — depth-from-pia axial feature.
16	- `benchmark_analysis.anisotropic_similarity_fit()` — Procrustes rotation + per-axis scale + translation.
17	- `manual workflow/manual_coreg_utils.py`: `choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor()` (k-NN + max-density, duplicate-safe), `one_to_one_matching()` (iterative global-min-distance), `get_ids_from_landmarks()`.
18	- `manual workflow/landmark_filtering.py`: `grid_sample_landmarks()`, volume bounds, distribution viz.
19	- `scipy.interpolate.Rbf(..., function='thin_plate')` — TPS (per-axis, same as manual pipeline uses).
20	
21	**Benchmark priors (hard-codeable for init):**
22	- XY expansion ~1.77× (range 1.64–1.92×); Z expansion ~2.83× (range 2.13–3.58×).
23	- Rotation ~180° in XY on all 6 subjects.
24	- 1-NN ~20 µm; 5-NN ~33–37 µm.
25	- Post-affine residual 16–43 µm RMS — the scale of the nonrigid warp TPS must handle.
26	- Manual match rates 34–86 % define the per-subject difficulty ceiling.
27	
28	**Landmark CSV ground truth (no header, 8 cols):**
29	`ids, active, cz_x, cz_y, cz_z, hcr_x, hcr_y, hcr_z`
30	`Pt-{n}` rows = manual landmarks (lowest Pt-# = initial constellation, 4–6 cells clustered in (x,y,z), few other ROIs in convex hull); `cz#-hcr#` rows = automatic candidates awaiting QC.
31	
32	## What is NOT yet built (the gaps the grand plan organises)
33	
34	- Sub-volume localisation (CZ inside HCR).
35	- Constellation detector + initial-match finder.
36	- Iterative TPS landmark expansion with automatic confidence gate.
37	- ROI-to-ROI classification / QC scorer.
38	- **Manual-correction GUI** for reviewing automated results and generating training labels (currently only BigDataViewer/FIJI is used; a bespoke GUI is needed because automated steps must be auditable and correctable, and every correction becomes a training sample).
39	- End-to-end pipeline that wires the above into a `coreg_table.csv` writer.
40	
41	## QC training data — sources of ground truth
42	
43	The grand plan treats these three sources as the QC supervision signal:
44	
45	1. **`coreg_table.csv`** — the canonical gold standard per subject (one-to-one `[czstack_id, hcr_id]`, output of manual workflow step 4). Primary positives for training and for final metrics.
46	2. **`landmarks_*_qced.csv` rows with `active=true`** — richer superset of coreg_table (contains intermediate QC'd pairs); also positives.
47	3. **GUI-generated corrections** — every accept/reject/adjust action becomes a labelled example. This is the only way the training set grows over time, and it is why the GUI is a first-class deliverable rather than an afterthought.
48	
49	Negatives are random unmatched pairs within spatial neighbourhoods; hard negatives come from candidates that the GUI user rejected.
50	
51	## Deliverable
52	
53	- **Path:** `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` (next number in the existing `01..06` doc sequence, matching the spaces-and-title-case naming).
54	- **Status contract:** each candidate block ends with a Status line — `not_started | in_progress | validated | abandoned` — plus a one-line Findings summary and a pointer to the session's `log.md` once closed. Future sessions edit the Status line when they pick up / finish a candidate.
55	- **One-line reference added to `CLAUDE.md`** under the existing "Project References" list so it shows up alongside `01..06`.
56	
57	## Structure of the grand plan markdown
58	
59	```
60	# Grand plan — automated czstack ↔ HCR coregistration
61	
62	## 0. Foundations already available   (pointers only; no content duplication)
63	
64	## 1. Registration protocol
65	   Pipeline sketch:  Surface → Coarse affine → Seed match → TPS expansion → Fine match
66	   1.A  Coarse localisation   (R1)
67	   1.B  Seed match            (R2)    ← requires QC gate (Q0 or Q1)
68	   1.C  Landmark expansion    (R3)    ← requires QC gate (Q1 or Q2)
69	   1.D  Optional upgrades     (R4–R6)
70	
71	## 2. QC protocol (ROI-to-ROI confirmation)
72	   2.A  Distance + context filters   (Q1)                    — gate for R3
73	   2.B  Seed-match verifier          (Q0, small/special)     — gate for R2
74	   2.C  Hand-crafted feature scorer  (Q2, Q3)
75	   2.D  Learned embeddings           (Q4, Q5)
76	
77	## 3. GUI for review & correction      (G1 basic, G2 active-learning loop)
78	   3.A  Minimum viable viewer + accept/reject/adjust
79	   3.B  Exports updated landmarks.csv and coreg_table.csv
80	   3.C  Every action is logged as a labelled training example for Q3/Q4/Q5
81	
82	## 4. Validation strategy
83	   Hold-out policy, metrics, dev-vs-benchmark split.
84	
85	## 5. Session / result ledger
86	   Table:  candidate | session dir | status | headline metric | link to log.md
87	```
88	
89	Each candidate block is short and uniform — **Goal / Inputs / Method sketch / Outputs / Success metric / Dependencies / Estimated effort / Status**.
90	
91	## Candidate list (ordered by simplicity × success likelihood)
92	
93	### Registration
94	
95	**R1 — Depth-profile + XY-density coarse alignment  _(tier 1, simplest)_**
96	- Apply anisotropic rescale + 180° prior to CZ GFP+ → approximate HCR space; align axially via 1D cross-correlation of depth-from-pia density profiles; align XY via grid search over 2D Gaussian-blurred GFP+ density maps.
97	- Outputs: coarse (R, S, t) — sub-volume localisation.
98	- Reuses: `estimate_pia_surface_*`, `depth_profile`, benchmark priors.
99	- Why first: all inputs exist; failure modes few.
100	
101	**R2 — Constellation matching (geometric-hash / graph seed)  _(tier 1; depends on Q0 / Q1 gate)_**
102	- In CZ, enumerate candidate 4–6 cell clusters ranked by "convex-hull emptiness" and tight spacing; compute invariant descriptor (pairwise-distance histogram, angles); search matching descriptor in HCR inside R1's coarse region; RANSAC-verify.
103	- Outputs: 4–6 seed active landmarks **plus a confidence score** — the top-K candidate seeds are surfaced for QC, not just the single best.
104	- Reuses: R1 output, `scipy.spatial.ConvexHull`, `scipy.spatial.distance_matrix`.
105	- Directly mimics the manual "initial constellation" step; cluster-uniqueness signature (user-confirmed) is the load-bearing cue.
106	- **QC dependency:** the automatic top-1 may be wrong; a seed-match verifier (Q0) or GUI pass must confirm before expansion, otherwise a bad seed propagates.
107	
108	**R3 — Iterative landmark expansion with TPS  _(tier 1, faithful manual mimic; depends on Q1/Q2 gate)_**
109	- Seeded by R2 (or by GUI-selected seed). Per-axis `Rbf` TPS on active landmarks; for each unmatched CZ cell → map to HCR → k-NN among GFP+ (reuse `choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor`, `k=5`, `feature='density'`) → accept if confidence gate passes → re-fit → repeat.
110	- Outputs: dense landmarks + candidate coreg table **with per-pair confidence**.
111	- Reuses: `manual_coreg_utils.*`, `Rbf`.
112	- **QC dependency:** the confidence gate is Q1 (tier 1) or Q2/Q3 (richer); without it, greedy expansion drifts. The landmark set should be **reviewable in the GUI** so a human can catch drift early.
113	
114	**R4 — Anisotropic ICP + grid-TPS refinement  _(tier 2)_**
115	- Anisotropic-scale ICP on all GFP+ seeded from R1/R2; grid TPS control points post-convergence. Tighter global fit than R3 but less robust to outliers.
116	
117	**R5 — Probabilistic nonrigid point-cloud registration (CPD / FilterReg)  _(tier 3)_**
118	- Off-the-shelf GMM-based nonrigid; native handling of partial overlap; parameter-heavy.
119	
120	**R6 — Learned descriptor / GNN / contrastive embedding  _(tier 3, research lane)_**
121	- Neighbourhood encoder → bipartite matching in embedding space; needs the R3+Q3 pipeline to produce training data first.
122	
123	### QC
124	
125	**Q0 — Seed-match verifier  _(tier 1; gates R2)_**
126	- Specialised check for the initial 4–6 pair constellation: does the CZ cluster's distance matrix match the candidate HCR cluster's within the known anisotropic expansion? Does the seed TPS (after anisotropic affine) have low residual on held-out nearby cells? If the top-1 fails, surface top-K via GUI.
127	- Outputs: accept / reject / human-review flag on the seed.
128	
129	**Q1 — Distance + context-consistency filter  _(tier 1; gates R3)_**
130	- Reject a candidate pair if residual > 3·σ of local active-landmark residuals, or if 1-NN-graph topology disagrees (CZ neighbours map near HCR neighbours). Direct confidence gate for R3's expansion loop.
131	
132	**Q2 — Neighbourhood descriptor scoring  _(tier 1)_**
133	- Per-candidate descriptor (sorted k-NN distances, relative angles, local density, depth-from-surface, ROI volume ratio, spot count / intensity); kernel similarity; single threshold. Cheap, auditable upgrade over Q1.
134	
135	**Q3 — Gradient-boosted classifier on hand-crafted features  _(tier 2; needs labelled data)_**
136	- **Training labels:**
137	  - Positives = `coreg_table.csv` pairs (canonical ground truth, output of manual step 4) **plus** `active=true` rows from the final iter-N landmarks CSV (supersets coreg_table with intermediate QC'd pairs).
138	  - Hard negatives = GUI-rejected candidates (grows over time).
139	  - Easy negatives = random unmatched pairs within spatial neighbourhoods.
140	- Features from Q2; leave-one-subject-out CV; calibrated match probability. 500–1000 positives per subject × 6 subjects is sufficient for tree methods.
141	- Replaces the hand-tuned threshold in Q1/Q2 with a learned one.
142	
143	**Q4 — Contrastive neighbourhood embedding  _(tier 3)_**
144	- Small PointNet / Transformer on local point clouds; InfoNCE loss pairing `coreg_table` matches. Training data same sources as Q3.
145	
146	**Q5 — GAN-based match classification  _(tier 3, user-mentioned)_**
147	- Discriminator between matched vs. unmatched neighbourhood patches; generator for data augmentation. Included for completeness; expected lower ROI than Q3/Q4 at this data scale.
148	
149	### GUI  (first-class deliverable — enables all of QC training and active learning)
150	
151	**G1 — Minimum viable review GUI  _(tier 1; blocks Q3/Q4/Q5 training)_**
152	- Load CZ z-stack + HCR volume + current landmarks + candidate matches.
153	- Side-by-side or overlaid viewer (candidate: [napari](https://napari.org) with a plugin, or a Jupyter/ipywidgets panel — decide in the first G1 session).
154	- Per-candidate: accept / reject / adjust HCR target / flag uncertain.
155	- Writes updated `landmarks_*_qced.csv` and regenerates `coreg_table.csv` via manual step 4 logic.
156	- **Every GUI action is appended to a `qc_actions.jsonl` log** (timestamp, subject, candidate_id, action, before/after coordinates) — this is the training corpus for Q3/Q4/Q5 and for future active-learning rounds.
157	
158	**G2 — Active-learning loop  _(tier 2; depends on G1 + Q3)_**
159	- Given a trained Q3 classifier, surface the highest-uncertainty candidates first; human labels fastest-growing signal; retrain Q3 periodically.
160	
161	## Ordering rationale (written into the doc)
162	
163	- Simplicity first: R1 → R2 → R3 all reuse existing utilities and can land in separate small sessions.
164	- **QC and GUI are not "after" registration — they are co-requisites.** R2 requires Q0 (seed verifier) or a GUI pass to avoid seeding from a wrong cluster; R3 requires Q1 (or richer gate) to avoid greedy drift. Concretely the recommended session order is:
165	  1. R1 (coarse align; no QC dependency)
166	  2. Q1 + Q2 (gate logic; ready for R3 as soon as a seed exists)
167	  3. G1 (minimum GUI; unblocks Q3 training data collection)
168	  4. R2 with Q0 (seed match + verifier; GUI surfaces top-K if top-1 is rejected)
169	  5. R3 gated by Q1/Q2 (expansion)
170	  6. Q3 on accumulated labels from G1 actions, upgrading the R3 gate
171	  7. R4/R5 and Q4/Q5/G2 as later rounds
172	- Most of the between-modality variance is explained by the anisotropic affine + surface-plane alignment (benchmark stats); the remaining ~25–45 µm residual is what TPS on ~50–100 landmarks handles in the manual protocol.
173	- ML/deep tiers last: they need `coreg_table.csv` + GUI-generated labels; R3+Q3+G1 together bootstrap that corpus.
174	
175	## Validation strategy (same for every candidate, written into the doc)
176	
177	- Respect CLAUDE.md dev-protocol rule: **do not use benchmark data to design the algorithms**; use it only to validate afterwards.
178	- Primary validation subjects: 788406, 790322 (high-quality spot data).
179	- Stress subjects: 767018 (older pipeline, 35 % match rate) and 782149 (thin + tilted, 34 %).
180	- Secondary: 755252, 767022 (intensity-only GFP+ — needs threshold sweep).
181	- Metrics:
182	  - Registration: % of CZ cells within D µm of their manual match (D ∈ {5, 10, 20}); landmark residual RMS; match-rate vs. manual baseline.
183	  - QC: precision/recall vs. manual `active=true` labels; calibration plot.
184	
185	## Session / ledger template (written into the doc)
186	
187	| Candidate | Session dir                       | Status      | Headline metric | Log |
188	|-----------|-----------------------------------|-------------|-----------------|-----|
189	| R1        | `sessions/NN_R1_coarse_align/`    | not_started | —               | —   |
190	| Q1        | `sessions/NN_Q1_gate/`            | not_started | —               | —   |
191	| G1        | `sessions/NN_G1_review_gui/`      | not_started | —               | —   |
192	| R2 + Q0   | `sessions/NN_R2_constellation/`   | not_started | —               | —   |
193	| R3        | `sessions/NN_R3_tps_expansion/`   | not_started | —               | —   |
194	| Q3        | `sessions/NN_Q3_classifier/`      | not_started | —               | —   |
195	| …         |                                   |             |                 |     |
196	
197	## Verification of the grand plan after writing
198	
199	1. `Grep` `CLAUDE.md` for "07" to confirm the one-line link landed in the Project References block.
200	2. Open `07 Grand Plan.md` and confirm every candidate block carries the same fields (Goal/Inputs/Method/Outputs/Success metric/Dependencies/Effort/Status).
201	3. Ordering check: R1 precedes R2 precedes R3; Q1 precedes Q2 precedes Q3; tiers monotonically increase.
202	4. Sanity read: a new session picking up R1 should be able to start without reading any other doc besides 01–07 and the referenced utility functions.
203	
204	## Files touched during execution
205	
206	- `CREATE` `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`
207	- `EDIT`  `/root/capsule/CLAUDE.md` — add one line under "Project References":
208	  `- /root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` (the living candidate-ordered plan)

# Plan — create the "grand plan" markdown for automated czstack ↔ HCR coregistration

## Context

The project goal is to automate the coregistration that is currently manual (BigWarp). Prior sessions have built solid foundations (benchmark loader, pia surface estimation, anisotropy/expansion analysis, depth-from-surface coordinate) but **no automated registration candidate has been implemented yet**. The user wants a **persistent markdown** that enumerates candidate approaches, ordered by simplicity × likely success rate, so future sessions pick them up one by one and update results in-place.

This plan file is a meta-plan. The deliverable is a new doc in the repo — ordering, candidate sketches, reuse pointers, validation rules — that becomes the single source of truth for what to attempt next.

## What the grand plan builds on (verified during this session)

**Production-ready utilities (under `dev_code/` and `manual workflow/`):**
- `benchmark_data_loader.load_subject()` — unified loader; handles 3 GFP+ sources (spot-count CSV, raw spot aggregation, intensity CSV).
- `benchmark_analysis.estimate_pia_surface_quantile_ceiling()` — canonical HCR pia surface (N22 default, validated on all 6 subjects, `above_frac ≤ 0.08 %`, `onset_depth 2.5–7.5 µm`).
- `benchmark_analysis.estimate_pia_surface_image_ceiling()` — CZ image-based surface (roughness ≤ 12 µm, tilt ≤ 3°).
- `benchmark_analysis.depth_from_surface()`, `depth_profile()` — depth-from-pia axial feature.
- `benchmark_analysis.anisotropic_similarity_fit()` — Procrustes rotation + per-axis scale + translation.
- `manual workflow/manual_coreg_utils.py`: `choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor()` (k-NN + max-density, duplicate-safe), `one_to_one_matching()` (iterative global-min-distance), `get_ids_from_landmarks()`.
- `manual workflow/landmark_filtering.py`: `grid_sample_landmarks()`, volume bounds, distribution viz.
- `scipy.interpolate.Rbf(..., function='thin_plate')` — TPS (per-axis, same as manual pipeline uses).

**Benchmark priors (hard-codeable for init):**
- XY expansion ~1.77× (range 1.64–1.92×); Z expansion ~2.83× (range 2.13–3.58×).
- Rotation ~180° in XY on all 6 subjects.
- 1-NN ~20 µm; 5-NN ~33–37 µm.
- Post-affine residual 16–43 µm RMS — the scale of the nonrigid warp TPS must handle.
- Manual match rates 34–86 % define the per-subject difficulty ceiling.

**Landmark CSV ground truth (no header, 8 cols):**
`ids, active, cz_x, cz_y, cz_z, hcr_x, hcr_y, hcr_z`
`Pt-{n}` rows = manual landmarks (lowest Pt-# = initial constellation, 4–6 cells clustered in (x,y,z), few other ROIs in convex hull); `cz#-hcr#` rows = automatic candidates awaiting QC.

## What is NOT yet built (the gaps the grand plan organises)

- Sub-volume localisation (CZ inside HCR).
- Constellation detector + initial-match finder.
- Iterative TPS landmark expansion with automatic confidence gate.
- ROI-to-ROI classification / QC scorer.
- **Manual-correction GUI** for reviewing automated results and generating training labels (currently only BigDataViewer/FIJI is used; a bespoke GUI is needed because automated steps must be auditable and correctable, and every correction becomes a training sample).
- End-to-end pipeline that wires the above into a `coreg_table.csv` writer.

## QC training data — sources of ground truth

The grand plan treats these three sources as the QC supervision signal:

1. **`coreg_table.csv`** — the canonical gold standard per subject (one-to-one `[czstack_id, hcr_id]`, output of manual workflow step 4). Primary positives for training and for final metrics.
2. **`landmarks_*_qced.csv` rows with `active=true`** — richer superset of coreg_table (contains intermediate QC'd pairs); also positives.
3. **GUI-generated corrections** — every accept/reject/adjust action becomes a labelled example. This is the only way the training set grows over time, and it is why the GUI is a first-class deliverable rather than an afterthought.

Negatives are random unmatched pairs within spatial neighbourhoods; hard negatives come from candidates that the GUI user rejected.

## Deliverable

- **Path:** `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` (next number in the existing `01..06` doc sequence, matching the spaces-and-title-case naming).
- **Status contract:** each candidate block ends with a Status line — `not_started | in_progress | validated | abandoned` — plus a one-line Findings summary and a pointer to the session's `log.md` once closed. Future sessions edit the Status line when they pick up / finish a candidate.
- **One-line reference added to `CLAUDE.md`** under the existing "Project References" list so it shows up alongside `01..06`.

## Structure of the grand plan markdown

```
# Grand plan — automated czstack ↔ HCR coregistration

## 0. Foundations already available   (pointers only; no content duplication)

## 1. Registration protocol
   Pipeline sketch:  Surface → Coarse affine → Seed match → TPS expansion → Fine match
   1.A  Coarse localisation   (R1)
   1.B  Seed match            (R2)    ← requires QC gate (Q0 or Q1)
   1.C  Landmark expansion    (R3)    ← requires QC gate (Q1 or Q2)
   1.D  Optional upgrades     (R4–R6)

## 2. QC protocol (ROI-to-ROI confirmation)
   2.A  Distance + context filters   (Q1)                    — gate for R3
   2.B  Seed-match verifier          (Q0, small/special)     — gate for R2
   2.C  Hand-crafted feature scorer  (Q2, Q3)
   2.D  Learned embeddings           (Q4, Q5)

## 3. GUI for review & correction      (G1 basic, G2 active-learning loop)
   3.A  Minimum viable viewer + accept/reject/adjust
   3.B  Exports updated landmarks.csv and coreg_table.csv
   3.C  Every action is logged as a labelled training example for Q3/Q4/Q5

## 4. Validation strategy
   Hold-out policy, metrics, dev-vs-benchmark split.

## 5. Session / result ledger
   Table:  candidate | session dir | status | headline metric | link to log.md
```

Each candidate block is short and uniform — **Goal / Inputs / Method sketch / Outputs / Success metric / Dependencies / Estimated effort / Status**.

## Candidate list (ordered by simplicity × success likelihood)

### Registration

**R1 — Depth-profile + XY-density coarse alignment  _(tier 1, simplest)_**
- Apply anisotropic rescale + 180° prior to CZ GFP+ → approximate HCR space; align axially via 1D cross-correlation of depth-from-pia density profiles; align XY via grid search over 2D Gaussian-blurred GFP+ density maps.
- Outputs: coarse (R, S, t) — sub-volume localisation.
- Reuses: `estimate_pia_surface_*`, `depth_profile`, benchmark priors.
- Why first: all inputs exist; failure modes few.

**R2 — Constellation matching (geometric-hash / graph seed)  _(tier 1; depends on Q0 / Q1 gate)_**
- In CZ, enumerate candidate 4–6 cell clusters ranked by "convex-hull emptiness" and tight spacing; compute invariant descriptor (pairwise-distance histogram, angles); search matching descriptor in HCR inside R1's coarse region; RANSAC-verify.
- Outputs: 4–6 seed active landmarks **plus a confidence score** — the top-K candidate seeds are surfaced for QC, not just the single best.
- Reuses: R1 output, `scipy.spatial.ConvexHull`, `scipy.spatial.distance_matrix`.
- Directly mimics the manual "initial constellation" step; cluster-uniqueness signature (user-confirmed) is the load-bearing cue.
- **QC dependency:** the automatic top-1 may be wrong; a seed-match verifier (Q0) or GUI pass must confirm before expansion, otherwise a bad seed propagates.

**R3 — Iterative landmark expansion with TPS  _(tier 1, faithful manual mimic; depends on Q1/Q2 gate)_**
- Seeded by R2 (or by GUI-selected seed). Per-axis `Rbf` TPS on active landmarks; for each unmatched CZ cell → map to HCR → k-NN among GFP+ (reuse `choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor`, `k=5`, `feature='density'`) → accept if confidence gate passes → re-fit → repeat.
- Outputs: dense landmarks + candidate coreg table **with per-pair confidence**.
- Reuses: `manual_coreg_utils.*`, `Rbf`.
- **QC dependency:** the confidence gate is Q1 (tier 1) or Q2/Q3 (richer); without it, greedy expansion drifts. The landmark set should be **reviewable in the GUI** so a human can catch drift early.

**R4 — Anisotropic ICP + grid-TPS refinement  _(tier 2)_**
- Anisotropic-scale ICP on all GFP+ seeded from R1/R2; grid TPS control points post-convergence. Tighter global fit than R3 but less robust to outliers.

**R5 — Probabilistic nonrigid point-cloud registration (CPD / FilterReg)  _(tier 3)_**
- Off-the-shelf GMM-based nonrigid; native handling of partial overlap; parameter-heavy.

**R6 — Learned descriptor / GNN / contrastive embedding  _(tier 3, research lane)_**
- Neighbourhood encoder → bipartite matching in embedding space; needs the R3+Q3 pipeline to produce training data first.

### QC

**Q0 — Seed-match verifier  _(tier 1; gates R2)_**
- Specialised check for the initial 4–6 pair constellation: does the CZ cluster's distance matrix match the candidate HCR cluster's within the known anisotropic expansion? Does the seed TPS (after anisotropic affine) have low residual on held-out nearby cells? If the top-1 fails, surface top-K via GUI.
- Outputs: accept / reject / human-review flag on the seed.

**Q1 — Distance + context-consistency filter  _(tier 1; gates R3)_**
- Reject a candidate pair if residual > 3·σ of local active-landmark residuals, or if 1-NN-graph topology disagrees (CZ neighbours map near HCR neighbours). Direct confidence gate for R3's expansion loop.

**Q2 — Neighbourhood descriptor scoring  _(tier 1)_**
- Per-candidate descriptor (sorted k-NN distances, relative angles, local density, depth-from-surface, ROI volume ratio, spot count / intensity); kernel similarity; single threshold. Cheap, auditable upgrade over Q1.

**Q3 — Gradient-boosted classifier on hand-crafted features  _(tier 2; needs labelled data)_**
- **Training labels:**
  - Positives = `coreg_table.csv` pairs (canonical ground truth, output of manual step 4) **plus** `active=true` rows from the final iter-N landmarks CSV (supersets coreg_table with intermediate QC'd pairs).
  - Hard negatives = GUI-rejected candidates (grows over time).
  - Easy negatives = random unmatched pairs within spatial neighbourhoods.
- Features from Q2; leave-one-subject-out CV; calibrated match probability. 500–1000 positives per subject × 6 subjects is sufficient for tree methods.
- Replaces the hand-tuned threshold in Q1/Q2 with a learned one.

**Q4 — Contrastive neighbourhood embedding  _(tier 3)_**
- Small PointNet / Transformer on local point clouds; InfoNCE loss pairing `coreg_table` matches. Training data same sources as Q3.

**Q5 — GAN-based match classification  _(tier 3, user-mentioned)_**
- Discriminator between matched vs. unmatched neighbourhood patches; generator for data augmentation. Included for completeness; expected lower ROI than Q3/Q4 at this data scale.

### GUI  (first-class deliverable — enables all of QC training and active learning)

**G1 — Minimum viable review GUI  _(tier 1; blocks Q3/Q4/Q5 training)_**
- Load CZ z-stack + HCR volume + current landmarks + candidate matches.
- Side-by-side or overlaid viewer (candidate: [napari](https://napari.org) with a plugin, or a Jupyter/ipywidgets panel — decide in the first G1 session).
- Per-candidate: accept / reject / adjust HCR target / flag uncertain.
- Writes updated `landmarks_*_qced.csv` and regenerates `coreg_table.csv` via manual step 4 logic.
- **Every GUI action is appended to a `qc_actions.jsonl` log** (timestamp, subject, candidate_id, action, before/after coordinates) — this is the training corpus for Q3/Q4/Q5 and for future active-learning rounds.

**G2 — Active-learning loop  _(tier 2; depends on G1 + Q3)_**
- Given a trained Q3 classifier, surface the highest-uncertainty candidates first; human labels fastest-growing signal; retrain Q3 periodically.

## Ordering rationale (written into the doc)

- Simplicity first: R1 → R2 → R3 all reuse existing utilities and can land in separate small sessions.
- **QC and GUI are not "after" registration — they are co-requisites.** R2 requires Q0 (seed verifier) or a GUI pass to avoid seeding from a wrong cluster; R3 requires Q1 (or richer gate) to avoid greedy drift. Concretely the recommended session order is:
  1. R1 (coarse align; no QC dependency)
  2. Q1 + Q2 (gate logic; ready for R3 as soon as a seed exists)
  3. G1 (minimum GUI; unblocks Q3 training data collection)
  4. R2 with Q0 (seed match + verifier; GUI surfaces top-K if top-1 is rejected)
  5. R3 gated by Q1/Q2 (expansion)
  6. Q3 on accumulated labels from G1 actions, upgrading the R3 gate
  7. R4/R5 and Q4/Q5/G2 as later rounds
- Most of the between-modality variance is explained by the anisotropic affine + surface-plane alignment (benchmark stats); the remaining ~25–45 µm residual is what TPS on ~50–100 landmarks handles in the manual protocol.
- ML/deep tiers last: they need `coreg_table.csv` + GUI-generated labels; R3+Q3+G1 together bootstrap that corpus.

## Validation strategy (same for every candidate, written into the doc)

- Respect CLAUDE.md dev-protocol rule: **do not use benchmark data to design the algorithms**; use it only to validate afterwards.
- Primary validation subjects: 788406, 790322 (high-quality spot data).
- Stress subjects: 767018 (older pipeline, 35 % match rate) and 782149 (thin + tilted, 34 %).
- Secondary: 755252, 767022 (intensity-only GFP+ — needs threshold sweep).
- Metrics:
  - Registration: % of CZ cells within D µm of their manual match (D ∈ {5, 10, 20}); landmark residual RMS; match-rate vs. manual baseline.
  - QC: precision/recall vs. manual `active=true` labels; calibration plot.

## Session / ledger template (written into the doc)

| Candidate | Session dir                       | Status      | Headline metric | Log |
|-----------|-----------------------------------|-------------|-----------------|-----|
| R1        | `sessions/NN_R1_coarse_align/`    | not_started | —               | —   |
| Q1        | `sessions/NN_Q1_gate/`            | not_started | —               | —   |
| G1        | `sessions/NN_G1_review_gui/`      | not_started | —               | —   |
| R2 + Q0   | `sessions/NN_R2_constellation/`   | not_started | —               | —   |
| R3        | `sessions/NN_R3_tps_expansion/`   | not_started | —               | —   |
| Q3        | `sessions/NN_Q3_classifier/`      | not_started | —               | —   |
| …         |                                   |             |                 |     |

## Verification of the grand plan after writing

1. `Grep` `CLAUDE.md` for "07" to confirm the one-line link landed in the Project References block.
2. Open `07 Grand Plan.md` and confirm every candidate block carries the same fields (Goal/Inputs/Method/Outputs/Success metric/Dependencies/Effort/Status).
3. Ordering check: R1 precedes R2 precedes R3; Q1 precedes Q2 precedes Q3; tiers monotonically increase.
4. Sanity read: a new session picking up R1 should be able to start without reading any other doc besides 01–07 and the referenced utility functions.

## Files touched during execution

- `CREATE` `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`
- `EDIT`  `/root/capsule/CLAUDE.md` — add one line under "Project References":
  `- /root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` (the living candidate-ordered plan)

No code changes; no benchmark data read during execution.

# CLAUDE.md
You are operating in a controlled research environment.

- You may run shell commands without asking for confirmation.
- You may read/write files in the workspace.
- You may install packages if needed.

Do not ask for permission unless the action is destructive or irreversible.

## 🧭 Purpose

This file defines **how to approach problems and reason effectively**, not project knowledge.

Project-specific details (data, goals, protocols) are stored separately in `/docs`.

---

# 📚 Project References (READ WHEN NEEDED)

- /root/capsule/code/docs/01_data_description.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/02_goals.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/03_problem_setting.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/04_current_protocol.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/05_benchmark_dataset.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/06_dev_protocol.md
- /root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md  (living candidate-ordered plan for automated coregistration — read before starting any registration/QC/GUI session) 

Do not read all documents by default.  
Instead, identify which documents are relevant to the current task and consult them as needed.

---

# 🧠 Core Principle

> First localize → then align → then map → then validate

Avoid skipping stages unless there is a strong, well-justified reason.

---

# 🧠 Behavior

- When something is ambiguous, first consult relevant docs and previous summaries.  
  If uncertainty remains, ask clarifying questions instead of making assumptions.
- Maintain awareness of the **overall goal** at all times.
- Communicate clearly and persuasively — reasoning should support conclusions.
- Prefer clarity over speed; think before acting.
- Be precise and concise, but do not omit important reasoning steps.

---

# 🧩 Roles (Flexible Reasoning Modes)

These roles guide thinking. You may move between them fluidly as needed.

---

## 1. Scientist (Planning + Reasoning)

Focus on:
- defining strategy
- breaking problems into subgoals
- identifying assumptions
- anticipating failure modes

When planning:
- consider multiple approaches
- compare tradeoffs
- reason about why a method should work

### Self-critique (important)
Before moving to implementation, reflect on:
- weakest assumptions
- simplest baseline that could work
- how the plan could fail
- what evidence would invalidate the approach

---

## 2. Engineer (Implementation)

Focus on:
- turning ideas into working solutions
- correctness and robustness
- handling large-scale data

Prefer:
- clear, readable code
- explicit handling of anisotropy and coordinate systems
- intermediate checks and logging

Avoid:
- unnecessary complexity
- skipping validation steps

---

## 3. Evaluator (Validation)

Focus on:
- interpreting results
- identifying failure modes
- comparing approaches

Evaluate using:
- alignment quality
- neighborhood consistency
- mapping confidence

Look for:
- inconsistencies
- overfitting
- misleading improvements

---

## 4. Code Reviewer

Focus on:
- clarity and maintainability
- hidden assumptions
- robustness

Check:
- coordinate consistency
- reproducibility
- debuggability
- whether complexity is justified

---

## 5. Project Manager (Guiding + Chunking)

Focus on:
- aligning progress with the overall goal
- breaking work into manageable subgoals
- deciding when to continue, split, or summarize

### Consider starting a new thread when:
- switching pipeline stages
- trying a different method
- context becomes too large
- a benchmark phase completes

### Consider summarizing when:
- a subgoal is completed
- multiple approaches have been compared
- a failure pattern is understood
- results are sufficient to guide next steps

---

## 6. Notebook Summarizer

Focus on:
- compressing results into clear summaries
- preserving insights for future work

Each summary should capture:
- goal
- approach
- key results
- failures and lessons
- next steps

---

# 🔁 Workflow (Guideline)

A typical flow:

1. Plan (Scientist)
2. Reflect and critique
3. Implement (Engineer)
4. Review (Code Reviewer)
5. Evaluate (Evaluator)
6. Decide next step (Project Manager)
7. Summarize when appropriate

This flow can be adapted depending on the situation.

---

# 📦 Subgoal Definition

Each subgoal should clearly define:

- objective
- inputs
- expected outputs
- success criteria
- possible failure modes
- stopping condition

---

# 🔗 Handoff Concept

Treat each step as producing a structured summary:

- method used
- outputs generated
- evaluation results
- observed failure modes
- recommended next step

This allows work to continue cleanly across sessions.

---

# 📓 Logging

For each meaningful step, record:

- hypothesis
- method
- result
- failure modes
- next action

Do not rely on implicit memory.

---

# ⚠️ Anti-Patterns

Avoid:
- skipping localization without justification
- assuming perfect correspondence
- ignoring anisotropy
- overfitting to a single dataset
- ignoring or hiding failures
- continuing after clear evidence of failure

---

# 🧠 Reasoning Style

- structured and stepwise
- concise but complete
- explicit about uncertainty
- thoughtful before execution

---

# 🚀 Output Style

When appropriate, structure responses as:

**Plan**
- approach
- assumptions
- alternatives

**Reasoning**
- why this approach
- expected behavior
- potential failure modes

**Implementation (if needed)**
- clear and minimal

**Evaluation**
- results
- interpretation
- limitations

**Next Step**
- recommended direction

# Plan — Revise R1 (coarse affine) to be scale-free; add R1-B backup

## Context

The grand plan at `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` already exists. The user has now asked to **revise R1** because it relies on benchmark-derived expansion priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×), which:

1. Violates `06 Dev Protocol.md`: *"Do not use benchmark data information for developing automatic registration."*
2. Violates the grand plan's own opening rule: *"Do not consider parameter inference from the existing benchmark at all, except when explicitly stated."*
3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error); the failure modes are exactly the sub-populations the priors don't cover.

User constraints for the revised R1:
- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Add a backup plan** for when R1 fails.
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free)

Replace the current R1 block (around lines 64–77) with:

> **R1 — Scale-free coarse affine** _(tier 1)_
>
> - **Goal.** Recover an initial anisotropic affine `(R=180°XY, sx, sy, sz, tx, ty, tz)` using only (a) the 180° XY rotation prior and (b) the "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" prior. **No benchmark-derived scale priors.**
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate.
>   2. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   3. **XY initial translation.** Apply 180° XY rotation. Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   4. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   5. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   6. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S=diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation locked at 180° (no estimation here — residual rotation is R2's job).
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**
> - **Failure modes.**
>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
>   - Pia-surface fit errors propagate to `sz`.
>   - Identity-scale false peak if CZ profile is near-uniform.
>   - Cortical-layer mismatch if CZ spans only part of HCR's layers.
> - **Estimated effort.** 1–1.5 sessions.
> - **Status.** `to_be_revised` — current first-pass (`sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`, `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py`) is **superseded** because it used benchmark priors (XY ~1.77× / Z ~2.83× rescale and a GFP+-centroid XY trick). Z-profile 1D xcorr from the first pass is the only piece worth reusing.

### 2. Insert new candidate R1-B (human-anchor backup) after R1

> **R1-B — Backup coarse affine via G1 anchors** _(tier 1; invoked on low R1 confidence)_
>
> - **Goal.** When R1's confidence score falls below threshold (or a subject is flagged as hard), bootstrap coarse affine from 2–3 human-placed CZ↔HCR correspondences.
> - **Inputs.** 2–3 CZ↔HCR anchor correspondences placed via G1's "quick-anchor" mode (a small extension of the G1 adjust-target action).
> - **Method sketch.** Run `anisotropic_similarity_fit` on anchors.
>   - 2 anchors → rotation locked at 180°, isotropic scale only.
>   - 3+ anchors → anisotropic `(sx, sy, sz)` unlocked.
> - **Outputs.** Same `(R, S, t)` as R1.
> - **Success metric.** Same ≤ 100 µm origin criterion.
> - **Dependencies.** G1 quick-anchor mode.
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5 session (piggybacks on G1).
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 3. Insert new candidate R1-B2 (automatic fallback) after R1-B

> **R1-B2 — Automatic fallback: TEASER-scale-aware coarse estimate** _(tier 2; last-resort automatic)_
>
> - **Goal.** Fully automatic fallback when R1 confidence is low and no human anchors are available.
> - **Method sketch.** Generate feature-matched putative (CZ, HCR) correspondences (depth-from-surface + local density + volume similarity); run TEASER++ in scale-aware mode on the 180°-rotated putatives; use the inlier-certifiable `(R, S, t)` as the coarse affine.
> - **Dependencies.** `teaserpp_python` (shared with A1 infrastructure).
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5–1 session.
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 4. Downstream Dependencies line updates

Every candidate whose Dependencies line mentions "R1" now reads "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)":
- **R2** — line 91 currently "R1 (for region restriction); Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for region restriction; Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3."
- **R4** — line 116 "R1 + R2." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) + R2."
- **R5** — line 122 "R1 (for initial alignment)." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for initial alignment."
- **A1–A8** — each "R1." at lines 157, 171, 185, 194, 202, 208, 214, 220 → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)."

### 5. Section 0 — annotate benchmark priors as validation-only

Relabel the "Benchmark priors (hard-codeable for init)" subsection (around lines 37–43) to:

> ### Benchmark priors — **validation reference only**
>
> These numbers exist solely to check algorithm outputs against known benchmark statistics. **They must not seed any algorithm.** Our estimates of scale, rotation, residual, etc., are computed from data geometry; the priors are compared to those estimates only after the fact.
>
> - XY expansion ≈ 1.77× (range 1.64–1.92×).
> - Z expansion ≈ 2.83× (range 2.13–3.58×). Z:XY anisotropy ≈ 1.5×.
> - In-plane rotation ≈ 180° on all 6 subjects.   *(Exception: the 180° rotation is treated as a structural prior, consistent with the dev protocol — it has been verified across all 6 benchmark subjects and is an imaging-geometry constant, not a population statistic to learn from.)*
> - 1-NN cell spacing ≈ 20 µm; 5-NN ≈ 33–37 µm.
> - Post-affine landmark residual 16–43 µm RMS — this is the nonrigid warp scale TPS must absorb.

### 6. Section 1 intro update

Replace line 55 — "Two complementary tracks share the R1 coarse affine **and the G1 review GUI**." — with:

> Two complementary tracks share a coarse-affine stage (**R1**, with **R1-B** / **R1-B2** as fallbacks) **and the G1 review GUI**. They run in **parallel**, not in series — neither blocks the other.

### 7. Section 4 (Recommended session order) update

Under "Shared foundation (both tracks depend on these)" (around lines 321–324), replace with:

> 1. **R1** — scale-free coarse affine. Unblocks every downstream candidate on the easy subjects.
> 2. **G1** — minimum review GUI, including a **quick-anchor mode** (placing corresponding CZ↔HCR points). This is what R1-B depends on. Start in parallel with R1.
> 3. **R1-B** — human-anchor backup affine, wired up as soon as G1's quick-anchor mode lands. Used on any subject where R1's confidence is low.
> 4. **R1-B2** — optional automatic fallback; start only if R1 + R1-B still leave benchmark stress subjects uncovered after the first pass.

### 8. Section 6 (Ledger) rows

Insert two rows after the R1 row (line 384):

> `| R1-B | sessions/NN_R1B_anchor_backup/ | not_started | — | — |`
> `| R1-B2 | sessions/NN_R1B2_teaser_scale/ | not_started | — | — |`

### 9. Section 7 (Change log) entry

Append:

> - 2026-04-17 — **R1 revised to be scale-free.** Only 180° XY rotation and "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" priors are used; all expansion parameters are estimated from data with geometry-derived search bounds. First-pass implementation (`dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py` / `sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`) is marked superseded because it used benchmark priors. Added **R1-B** (G1-anchor backup, tier 1) and **R1-B2** (TEASER-scale-aware automatic fallback, tier 2). Updated Section 0 (benchmark priors → validation-reference only), Section 1 intro, downstream Dependencies lines (R2, R4, R5, A1–A8), Section 4 session order, Section 6 ledger.

## Files touched during execution

- **EDIT** `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` — all nine changes above.
- **No code touched.** Rewriting `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py` is the scope of the R1 revision session (opened in `sessions/NN_R1v2_scale_free/`), not of this planning task.
- **No benchmark data read.**

## Verification after edits

1. `Grep` the grand plan for `1.77` and `2.83` — they should appear **only** under Section 0's "validation reference only" heading; nowhere inside any R-/A-block method sketch.
2. `Grep` for `prior anisotropic rescale` — should be absent.
3. Every downstream Dependencies line that previously said `R1` should now read `R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)`.
4. R1-B and R1-B2 both carry the full candidate schema (Goal / Inputs / Method / Outputs / Success metric / Dependencies / Effort / Status).
5. Section 6 ledger has R1-B and R1-B2 rows.
6. Sanity read: a new R1-revision session can start from just 01–07 + the existing foundation utilities — no other context needed.

## Self-critique

- **Weakest new assumption:** CZ xy-centroid ≈ HCR xy-centroid. User stated "roughly"; mitigated by searching `(tx, ty)` over a ±half-HCR-extent box around the centroid guess.
- **Acknowledged risk:** R1 alone is expected to fail on the stress subjects (thin / asymmetric HCR, 782149 / 767022). That is exactly why R1-B is mandatory, not optional.
- **Alternative considered and rejected — moment / PCA-based scale.** Fragile to outliers; HCR regions are rectangular, not ellipsoidal; moments biased. Keeping density-based search.
- **Alternative considered and rejected — skip R1, always use A-series.** Drops the `CLAUDE.md` "first localise" principle; A-series still benefits from R1's HCR crop to reduce putative-correspondence count.
- **Failure of this plan would look like:** the data-derived `(sz, sxy)` bounds still produce a flat score surface on benchmark subjects even with the anisotropic refinement. In that case R1 confidence remains low → R1-B picks up the slack; that is the design, not a failure mode.

I have the context I need. Let me think through this carefully before writing the plan.

## What's wrong with the current R1

The current R1 uses benchmark-derived priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×). This violates both `06 Dev Protocol.md` ("Do not use benchmark data information for developing automatic registration") and the grand plan's own opening rule. The first-pass also swapped in a GFP+-centroid trick that's itself benchmark-calibrated (coverage 55–70 %). Current result: **3/6 pass origin, 3/6 fail** (782149 at 372 µm is the worst).

## Allowed priors for revised R1

Only:
1. **180° XY rotation** — validated by user across subjects, structural not statistical.
2. **CZ sits roughly at HCR XY center** — user's stated geometric assumption.

Scale must be **estimated from data** (Z from depth-profile cross-correlation, XY from density-map search), with data-derived search bounds (`L_hcr / L_cz`, not benchmark statistics).

## Backup strategy

R1 will fail on thin/asymmetric HCR sections (e.g. 782149). A backup that always works: **G1-anchor bootstrap** — user places 2–3 CZ↔HCR anchor points in the GUI, then `anisotropic_similarity_fit` on those. Secondary: **TEASER-scale-aware** (fully automatic, shares A1 infra).

## Downstream impact

Every candidate with "R1" in its Dependencies line (R2, R4, R5, A1–A8) gains "or R1-B / R1-B2". G1 gains a "quick-anchor" sub-feature. Section 0 benchmark priors get re-labelled as validation-only. No A-series / Q-series / G1-core logic changes.

Let me write the updated plan file.

# Plan — Revise R1 (coarse affine) to be scale-free; add R1-B backup

## Context

The grand plan at `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` already exists. The user has now asked to **revise R1** because it relies on benchmark-derived expansion priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×), which:

1. Violates `06 Dev Protocol.md`: *"Do not use benchmark data information for developing automatic registration."*
2. Violates the grand plan's own opening rule: *"Do not consider parameter inference from the existing benchmark at all, except when explicitly stated."*
3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error); the failure modes are exactly the sub-populations the priors don't cover.

User constraints for the revised R1:
- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Add a backup plan** for when R1 fails.
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free)

Replace the current R1 block (around lines 64–77) with:

> **R1 — Scale-free coarse affine** _(tier 1)_
>
> - **Goal.** Recover an initial anisotropic affine `(R=180°XY, sx, sy, sz, tx, ty, tz)` using only (a) the 180° XY rotation prior and (b) the "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" prior. **No benchmark-derived scale priors.**
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate.
>   2. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   3. **XY initial translation.** Apply 180° XY rotation. Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   4. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   5. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   6. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S=diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation locked at 180° (no estimation here — residual rotation is R2's job).
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**
> - **Failure modes.**
>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
>   - Pia-surface fit errors propagate to `sz`.
>   - Identity-scale false peak if CZ profile is near-uniform.
>   - Cortical-layer mismatch if CZ spans only part of HCR's layers.
> - **Estimated effort.** 1–1.5 sessions.
> - **Status.** `to_be_revised` — current first-pass (`sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`, `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py`) is **superseded** because it used benchmark priors (XY ~1.77× / Z ~2.83× rescale and a GFP+-centroid XY trick). Z-profile 1D xcorr from the first pass is the only piece worth reusing.

### 2. Insert new candidate R1-B (human-anchor backup) after R1

> **R1-B — Backup coarse affine via G1 anchors** _(tier 1; invoked on low R1 confidence)_
>
> - **Goal.** When R1's confidence score falls below threshold (or a subject is flagged as hard), bootstrap coarse affine from 2–3 human-placed CZ↔HCR correspondences.
> - **Inputs.** 2–3 CZ↔HCR anchor correspondences placed via G1's "quick-anchor" mode (a small extension of the G1 adjust-target action).
> - **Method sketch.** Run `anisotropic_similarity_fit` on anchors.
>   - 2 anchors → rotation locked at 180°, isotropic scale only.
>   - 3+ anchors → anisotropic `(sx, sy, sz)` unlocked.
> - **Outputs.** Same `(R, S, t)` as R1.
> - **Success metric.** Same ≤ 100 µm origin criterion.
> - **Dependencies.** G1 quick-anchor mode.
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5 session (piggybacks on G1).
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 3. Insert new candidate R1-B2 (automatic fallback) after R1-B

> **R1-B2 — Automatic fallback: TEASER-scale-aware coarse estimate** _(tier 2; last-resort automatic)_
>
> - **Goal.** Fully automatic fallback when R1 confidence is low and no human anchors are available.
> - **Method sketch.** Generate feature-matched putative (CZ, HCR) correspondences (depth-from-surface + local density + volume similarity); run TEASER++ in scale-aware mode on the 180°-rotated putatives; use the inlier-certifiable `(R, S, t)` as the coarse affine.
> - **Dependencies.** `teaserpp_python` (shared with A1 infrastructure).
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5–1 session.
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 4. Downstream Dependencies line updates

Every candidate whose Dependencies line mentions "R1" now reads "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)":
- **R2** — line 91 currently "R1 (for region restriction); Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for region restriction; Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3."
- **R4** — line 116 "R1 + R2." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) + R2."
- **R5** — line 122 "R1 (for initial alignment)." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for initial alignment."
- **A1–A8** — each "R1." at lines 157, 171, 185, 194, 202, 208, 214, 220 → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)."

### 5. Section 0 — annotate benchmark priors as validation-only

Relabel the "Benchmark priors (hard-codeable for init)" subsection (around lines 37–43) to:

> ### Benchmark priors — **validation reference only**
>
> These numbers exist solely to check algorithm outputs against known benchmark statistics. **They must not seed any algorithm.** Our estimates of scale, rotation, residual, etc., are computed from data geometry; the priors are compared to those estimates only after the fact.
>
> - XY expansion ≈ 1.77× (range 1.64–1.92×).
> - Z expansion ≈ 2.83× (range 2.13–3.58×). Z:XY anisotropy ≈ 1.5×.
> - In-plane rotation ≈ 180° on all 6 subjects.   *(Exception: the 180° rotation is treated as a structural prior, consistent with the dev protocol — it has been verified across all 6 benchmark subjects and is an imaging-geometry constant, not a population statistic to learn from.)*
> - 1-NN cell spacing ≈ 20 µm; 5-NN ≈ 33–37 µm.
> - Post-affine landmark residual 16–43 µm RMS — this is the nonrigid warp scale TPS must absorb.

### 6. Section 1 intro update

Replace line 55 — "Two complementary tracks share the R1 coarse affine **and the G1 review GUI**." — with:

> Two complementary tracks share a coarse-affine stage (**R1**, with **R1-B** / **R1-B2** as fallbacks) **and the G1 review GUI**. They run in **parallel**, not in series — neither blocks the other.

### 7. Section 4 (Recommended session order) update

Under "Shared foundation (both tracks depend on these)" (around lines 321–324), replace with:

> 1. **R1** — scale-free coarse affine. Unblocks every downstream candidate on the easy subjects.
> 2. **G1** — minimum review GUI, including a **quick-anchor mode** (placing corresponding CZ↔HCR points). This is what R1-B depends on. Start in parallel with R1.
> 3. **R1-B** — human-anchor backup affine, wired up as soon as G1's quick-anchor mode lands. Used on any subject where R1's confidence is low.
> 4. **R1-B2** — optional automatic fallback; start only if R1 + R1-B still leave benchmark stress subjects uncovered after the first pass.

### 8. Section 6 (Ledger) rows

Insert two rows after the R1 row (line 384):

> `| R1-B | sessions/NN_R1B_anchor_backup/ | not_started | — | — |`
> `| R1-B2 | sessions/NN_R1B2_teaser_scale/ | not_started | — | — |`

### 9. Section 7 (Change log) entry

First-pass implementation (`dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py` / `sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`) is marked superseded because it used benchmark priors. # Plan — Revise R1 (coarse affine) to be scale-free; add R1-B backup

## Context

The grand plan at `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` already exists. The user has now asked to **revise R1** because it relies on benchmark-derived expansion priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×), which:

1. Violates `06 Dev Protocol.md`: *"Do not use benchmark data information for developing automatic registration."*
2. Violates the grand plan's own opening rule: *"Do not consider parameter inference from the existing benchmark at all, except when explicitly stated."*
3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error); the failure modes are exactly the sub-populations the priors don't cover.

User constraints for the revised R1:
- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Add a backup plan** for when R1 fails.
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free)

Replace the current R1 block (around lines 64–77) with:

> **R1 — Scale-free coarse affine** _(tier 1)_
>
> - **Goal.** Recover an initial anisotropic affine `(R=180°XY, sx, sy, sz, tx, ty, tz)` using only (a) the 180° XY rotation prior and (b) the "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" prior. **No benchmark-derived scale priors.**
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate.
>   2. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   3. **XY initial translation.** Apply 180° XY rotation. Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   4. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   5. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   6. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S=diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation locked at 180° (no estimation here — residual rotation is R2's job).
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**
> - **Failure modes.**
>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
>   - Pia-surface fit errors propagate to `sz`.
>   - Identity-scale false peak if CZ profile is near-uniform.
>   - Cortical-layer mismatch if CZ spans only part of HCR's layers.
> - **Estimated effort.** 1–1.5 sessions.
> - **Status.** `to_be_revised` — current first-pass (`sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`, `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py`) is **superseded** because it used benchmark priors (XY ~1.77× / Z ~2.83× rescale and a GFP+-centroid XY trick). Z-profile 1D xcorr from the first pass is the only piece worth reusing.

### 2. Insert new candidate R1-B (human-anchor backup) after R1

> **R1-B — Backup coarse affine via G1 anchors** _(tier 1; invoked on low R1 confidence)_
>
> - **Goal.** When R1's confidence score falls below threshold (or a subject is flagged as hard), bootstrap coarse affine from 2–3 human-placed CZ↔HCR correspondences.
> - **Inputs.** 2–3 CZ↔HCR anchor correspondences placed via G1's "quick-anchor" mode (a small extension of the G1 adjust-target action).
> - **Method sketch.** Run `anisotropic_similarity_fit` on anchors.
>   - 2 anchors → rotation locked at 180°, isotropic scale only.
>   - 3+ anchors → anisotropic `(sx, sy, sz)` unlocked.
> - **Outputs.** Same `(R, S, t)` as R1.
> - **Success metric.** Same ≤ 100 µm origin criterion.
> - **Dependencies.** G1 quick-anchor mode.
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5 session (piggybacks on G1).
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 3. Insert new candidate R1-B2 (automatic fallback) after R1-B

> **R1-B2 — Automatic fallback: TEASER-scale-aware coarse estimate** _(tier 2; last-resort automatic)_
>
> - **Goal.** Fully automatic fallback when R1 confidence is low and no human anchors are available.
> - **Method sketch.** Generate feature-matched putative (CZ, HCR) correspondences (depth-from-surface + local density + volume similarity); run TEASER++ in scale-aware mode on the 180°-rotated putatives; use the inlier-certifiable `(R, S, t)` as the coarse affine.
> - **Dependencies.** `teaserpp_python` (shared with A1 infrastructure).
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5–1 session.
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

### 4. Downstream Dependencies line updates

Every candidate whose Dependencies line mentions "R1" now reads "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)":
- **R2** — line 91 currently "R1 (for region restriction); Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for region restriction; Q0 or GUI (G1) to verify before feeding R3."
- **R4** — line 116 "R1 + R2." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) + R2."
- **R5** — line 122 "R1 (for initial alignment)." → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2) for initial alignment."
- **A1–A8** — each "R1." at lines 157, 171, 185, 194, 202, 208, 214, 220 → "R1 (or R1-B / R1-B2)."

### 5. Section 0 — annotate benchmark priors as validation-only

Relabel the "Benchmark priors (hard-codeable for init)" subsection (around lines 37–43) to:

> ### Benchmark priors — **validation reference only**
>
> These numbers exist solely to check algorithm outputs against known benchmark statistics. **They must not seed any algorithm.** Our estimates of scale, rotation, residual, etc., are computed from data geometry; the priors are compared to those estimates only after the fact.
>
> - XY expansion ≈ 1.77× (range 1.64–1.92×).
> - Z expansion ≈ 2.83× (range 2.13–3.58×). Z:XY anisotropy ≈ 1.5×.
> - In-plane rotation ≈ 180° on all 6 subjects.   *(Exception: the 180° rotation is treated as a structural prior, consistent with the dev protocol — it has been verified across all 6 benchmark subjects and is an imaging-geometry constant, not a population statistic to learn from.)*
> - 1-NN cell spacing ≈ 20 µm; 5-NN ≈ 33–37 µm.
> - Post-affine landmark residual 16–43 µm RMS — this is the nonrigid warp scale TPS must absorb.

### 6. Section 1 intro update

Replace line 55 — "Two complementary tracks share the R1 coarse affine **and the G1 review GUI**." — with:

> Two complementary tracks share a coarse-affine stage (**R1**, with **R1-B** / **R1-B2** as fallbacks) **and the G1 review GUI**. They run in **parallel**, not in series — neither blocks the other.

### 7. Section 4 (Recommended session order) update

Under "Shared foundation (both tracks depend on these)" (around lines 321–324), replace with:

> 1. **R1** — scale-free coarse affine. Unblocks every downstream candidate on the easy subjects.
> 2. **G1** — minimum review GUI, including a **quick-anchor mode** (placing corresponding CZ↔HCR points). This is what R1-B depends on. Start in parallel with R1.
> 3. **R1-B** — human-anchor backup affine, wired up as soon as G1's quick-anchor mode lands. Used on any subject where R1's confidence is low.
> 4. **R1-B2** — optional automatic fallback; start only if R1 + R1-B still leave benchmark stress subjects uncovered after the first pass.

### 8. Section 6 (Ledger) rows

Insert two rows after the R1 row (line 384):

> `| R1-B | sessions/NN_R1B_anchor_backup/ | not_started | — | — |`
> `| R1-B2 | sessions/NN_R1B2_teaser_scale/ | not_started | — | — |`

### 9. Section 7 (Change log) entry

## Context

The grand plan at `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` already exists. The user has now asked to **revise R1** because it relies on benchmark-derived expansion priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×), which:

3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error).

User constraints for the revised R1:
- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Use the pia surface estimation step**: fit both pia surfaces as planes, match them linearly to recover the residual tilt (small rotation that composes with the 180° XY prior).
- **Backup plan must be automatic** (no human anchors).
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free, surface-tilt aligned)

Replace the current R1 block (around lines 64–77) with:

> **R1 — Scale-free coarse affine with surface-tilt alignment** _(tier 1)_
>
> - **Goal.** Recover an initial anisotropic affine `(R = R_tilt · R_180°XY, sx, sy, sz, tx, ty, tz)` using only (a) the 180° XY rotation prior, (b) the "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" prior, and (c) the pia-surface planes fitted from the existing surface-estimation utilities. **No benchmark-derived scale priors.**
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Pia-plane fit.** Fit a plane through each pia surface (existing `estimate_pia_surface_image_ceiling` for CZ and `estimate_pia_surface_quantile_ceiling` for HCR already give the surface points; pass those to a linear plane fit). Extract unit normals `n_cz`, `n_hcr`.
>   2. **Tilt-aligned rotation.** Apply `R_180°XY` to CZ, which rotates `n_cz → R_180°XY · n_cz`. Compute `R_tilt` as the small rotation (Rodrigues, axis = `(R_180°XY · n_cz) × n_hcr`, angle = `acos((R_180°XY · n_cz) · n_hcr)`) that aligns the rotated CZ normal with HCR's normal. Final rotation `R = R_tilt · R_180°XY`. On benchmark subjects the CZ tilt is ≤ 3°, so `R_tilt` is small and well-conditioned.
>   3. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate, consistent with the aligned surfaces.
>   4. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   5. **XY initial translation.** Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   6. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   7. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   8. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S = diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation `R` within ±5° of manual-landmark rotation.
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**
> - **Failure modes.**
>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
>   - Degenerate pia-plane fit (only happens if the surface fitter itself is already failing on that subject; then surface-based depth-from-surface is also unreliable).
>   - Identity-scale false peak if CZ profile is near-uniform.
>   - Cortical-layer mismatch if CZ spans only part of HCR's layers.
> - **Estimated effort.** 1–1.5 sessions.
> - **Status.** `to_be_revised` — current first-pass (`sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`, `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py`) is **superseded** because it used benchmark priors (XY ~1.77× / Z ~2.83× rescale and a GFP+-centroid XY trick). The Z-profile 1D xcorr from the first pass is the only piece worth reusing; tilt alignment and scale search are new.

### 2. Insert new candidate R1-B (automatic fallback) after R1

> **R1-B — Automatic fallback: TEASER-scale-aware coarse estimate** _(tier 2; invoked on low R1 confidence)_
>
> - **Goal.** When R1's confidence score falls below threshold, run a purely-automatic fallback to recover `(R, S, t)` on hard subjects (thin / asymmetric HCR, etc.).
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; per-cell features (depth-from-surface from R1's surface fit, local GFP+ density, ROI volume); `R_180°XY · R_tilt` from R1 step 1–2 (the tilt-aligned rotation is still reliable even if R1's scale/translation search fails — it only depends on the surface fits).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. Reuse `R = R_tilt · R_180°XY` from R1's surface-fit stage (cheap and reliable).
>   2. Generate feature-matched putative (CZ, HCR) correspondences using `(depth-from-surface, local density, volume)` similarity. Do **not** pre-apply any scale; TEASER handles scale internally.
>   3. Run TEASER++ in scale-aware mode (library: `teaserpp_python`) on the rotated putatives. Output: certifiable anisotropic `(S, t)` plus an inlier certificate.
>   4. If TEASER's inlier count is below a threshold, fall through to A1 / A2 / A3 with no crop (i.e., the A-series runs on the full HCR volume).
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S, t)` + inlier count as confidence.
> - **Success metric.** Same ≤ 100 µm origin criterion; intended to rescue the subjects where R1 alone fails.
> - **Dependencies.** R1's surface-fit stage (for `R`); `teaserpp_python` (shared with A1 infrastructure).
> - **Estimated effort.** 0.5–1 session (most of the machinery comes from A1).
> - **Status.** `not_started`.

# Plan — Revise R1 (coarse affine): scale-free, surface-tilt alignment, automatic-only backup

## Context

The grand plan at `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md` already exists. The user has now asked to **revise R1** because it relies on benchmark-derived expansion priors (XY ~1.77×, Z ~2.83×), which:

1. Violates `06 Dev Protocol.md`: *"Do not use benchmark data information for developing automatic registration."*
2. Violates the grand plan's own opening rule: *"Do not consider parameter inference from the existing benchmark at all, except when explicitly stated."*
3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error).

User constraints for the revised R1:
- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Use the pia surface estimation step**: fit both pia surfaces as planes, match them linearly to recover the residual tilt (small rotation that composes with the 180° XY prior).
- **Backup plan must be automatic** (no human anchors).
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free, surface-tilt aligned)

> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Pia-plane fit.** Fit a plane through each pia surface (existing `estimate_pia_surface_image_ceiling` for CZ and `estimate_pia_surface_quantile_ceiling` for HCR already give the surface points; pass those to a linear plane fit). Extract unit normals `n_cz`, `n_hcr`.
>   2. **Tilt-aligned rotation.** Apply `R_180°XY` to CZ, which rotates `n_cz → R_180°XY · n_cz`. Compute `R_tilt` as the small rotation (Rodrigues, axis = `(R_180°XY · n_cz) × n_hcr`, angle = `acos((R_180°XY · n_cz) · n_hcr)`) that aligns the rotated CZ normal with HCR's normal. Final rotation `R = R_tilt · R_180°XY`. On benchmark subjects the CZ tilt is ≤ 3°, so `R_tilt` is small and well-conditioned.
>   3. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate, consistent with the aligned surfaces.
>   4. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   5. **XY initial translation.** Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   6. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   7. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   8. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S = diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation `R` within ±5° of manual-landmark rotation.
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**
> - **Failure modes.**
>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
>   - Degenerate pia-plane fit (only happens if the surface fitter itself is already failing on that subject; then surface-based depth-from-surface is also unreliable).
>   - Identity-scale false peak if CZ profile is near-uniform.
>   - Cortical-layer mismatch if CZ spans only part of HCR's layers.
> - **Estimated effort.** 1–1.5 sessions.
> - **Status.** `to_be_revised` — current first-pass (`sessions/04_R1_coarse_align/`, `dev_code/r1_coarse_align.py`) is **superseded** because it used benchmark priors (XY ~1.77× / Z ~2.83× rescale and a GFP+-centroid XY trick). The Z-profile 1D xcorr from the first pass is the only piece worth reusing; tilt alignment and scale search are new.

### 2. Insert new candidate R1-B (automatic fallback) after R1

> **R1-B — Automatic fallback: TEASER-scale-aware coarse estimate** _(tier 2; invoked on low R1 confidence)_
>
> - **Goal.** When R1's confidence score falls below threshold, run a purely-automatic fallback to recover `(R, S, t)` on hard subjects (thin / asymmetric HCR, etc.).

## Context

3. Produced a first-pass that fails on 3/6 subjects (767018 136 µm, 767022 158 µm, 782149 372 µm origin error).

- **Only two priors allowed:** 180° XY rotation, and "CZ sits roughly at the XY center of HCR".
- **Nothing** may be assumed about expansion (XY or Z scales).
- **Use the pia surface estimation step**: fit both pia surfaces as planes, match them linearly to recover the residual tilt (small rotation that composes with the 180° XY prior).
- **Backup plan must be automatic** (no human anchors).
- Propagate the change to all downstream candidates that currently depend on R1.

## Changes to `/root/capsule/code/docs/07 Grand Plan.md`

### 1. Rewrite the R1 block (scale-free, surface-tilt aligned)

Replace the current R1 block (around lines 64–77) with:

> **R1 — Scale-free coarse affine with surface-tilt alignment** _(tier 1)_
>
> - **Goal.** Recover an initial anisotropic affine `(R = R_tilt · R_180°XY, sx, sy, sz, tx, ty, tz)` using only (a) the 180° XY rotation prior, (b) the "CZ ≈ HCR XY center" prior, and (c) the pia-surface planes fitted from the existing surface-estimation utilities. **No benchmark-derived scale priors.**
> - **Inputs.** CZ + HCR GFP+ centroids; both pia surfaces (foundation 0).
> - **Method sketch.**
>   1. **Pia-plane fit.** Fit a plane through each pia surface (existing `estimate_pia_surface_image_ceiling` for CZ and `estimate_pia_surface_quantile_ceiling` for HCR already give the surface points; pass those to a linear plane fit). Extract unit normals `n_cz`, `n_hcr`.
>   2. **Tilt-aligned rotation.** Apply `R_180°XY` to CZ, which rotates `n_cz → R_180°XY · n_cz`. Compute `R_tilt` as the small rotation (Rodrigues, axis = `(R_180°XY · n_cz) × n_hcr`, angle = `acos((R_180°XY · n_cz) · n_hcr)`) that aligns the rotated CZ normal with HCR's normal. Final rotation `R = R_tilt · R_180°XY`. On benchmark subjects the CZ tilt is ≤ 3°, so `R_tilt` is small and well-conditioned.
>   3. **Depth-from-surface** in both modalities — shared pia-anchored axial coordinate, consistent with the aligned surfaces.
>   4. **Z-scale + Z-offset search (1D).** Scan `sz ∈ [0.5, D_hcr / D_cz]` with step ≈ 0.02, where `D_cz, D_hcr` are the (95 %–5 %) depth extents of GFP+ cells in each modality (data-derived bounds). For each `sz`, rescale the CZ depth-density profile and cross-correlate with the HCR profile to find best `tz`. Pick `(sz, tz)` maximising NCC.
>   5. **XY initial translation.** Translate so CZ xy-centroid maps to HCR xy-centroid (the "roughly at center" prior).
>   6. **XY-scale + XY-translation joint search.** Scan isotropic `sxy ∈ [0.5, L_hcr / L_cz]` (step ≈ 0.05) and `(tx, ty)` within a ±half-HCR-XY-extent box on a sparse grid. For each `(sxy, tx, ty)`, rasterise Gaussian-blurred 2D density maps on a shared grid and score via NCC / NMI. Pick the best.
>   7. **Anisotropic refinement (optional).** Local search over `(sx, sy)` around the best `sxy` to allow axis-specific scales.
>   8. **Confidence.** Peak-to-RMS ratio of the score surface → scalar in `[0, ∞)`. Below a threshold (e.g. 3) → flag for R1-B.
> - **Outputs.** `(R, S = diag(sx, sy, sz), t)` + confidence score.
> - **Success metric.** Localised origin within ≤ 100 µm of manual-landmark-derived affine on held-out subjects; rotation `R` within ±5° of manual-landmark rotation.
> - **Dependencies.** Foundation 0 only (surfaces + depth profile). **No benchmark priors.**

>   - Thin / asymmetric HCR (e.g. 782149 case) → flat XY score surface.
